patterns_recognized: QA catch: HARD CAP 10's grade_reasons text cites a pick_prob that doesn't match the CSV's own pick_prob column on two rows tonight -- KC@BAL (text says 0.549, column is 0.5983) and LAA@MIN (text says 0.546, column is 0.5908) -- both are actually well outside the (0.50,0.55] band the cap is supposed to gate on. Flagged for the pipeline; doesn't change tonight's stake call on either game since HARD CAP 7 (KC@BAL) and HARD CAP 4 (LAA@MIN) independently and validly suppress both picks to do-not-parlay regardless of the pick_prob question.

Tonight's slate is unusually cap-10-saturated: 8 of 14 graded games (PHI@DET, NYY@WSH, KC@BAL, CLE@MIA, BOS@NYM, OAK@CHW, LAA@MIN, ATL@STL) fall in the (0.50,0.55] marginal-favorite pick_prob band and get zeroed by HARD CAP 10, leaving HOU@TEX as the only genuinely clean, actionable grade (A/score 5, no caps fired) on the board.

HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage) fires on 3 games tonight (KC@BAL, TOR@SD, COL@SF), consistent with its 0-for-7 validated backtest record; all three drop from pre-cap A/A-/B+ grades to B- or below and are treated as firm downgrades.

HARD CAP 1 (negative edge on an elevated tier) fires on ARI@LAD (DIAMOND, edge -9.09pp) -- Ohtani/LAD carries the strongest underlying signals of the night (PQI +40.8, SP edge +2.31 xERA) but the market has already priced it in, so there's no value. Textbook use case; confirmed, not re-litigated.

Two picks tonight fade home starters with strong documented recent form in the postgame archive -- Sandy Alcantara (3-1 for MIA, incl. 7-3 over WSH and 4-1 over TB) on CLE@MIA, and Nolan McLean (9-1 dominant win over CIN on 6/17) on BOS@NYM. Both reinforce rather than undercut HARD CAP 10's suppression; declining to resurrect CLE@MIA despite its pre-cap score of 5/A.

No platoon-brain or BvP batter context populated for any of tonight's 15 games (all *_batters_json / *_bvp_json fields are empty arrays) -- that layer of analysis was unavailable and played no role in tonight's reasoning.

Live statsapi check confirms all 30 probable starters match the diag CSV with no late scratches. CHC@CIN's Imanaga-vs-Greene matchup is now officially confirmed but remains correctly unscored per the thin-Statcast-sample gate (Greene at 89 pitches, needs 100+).

PHI @ DET.reasoning: DET is a thin pre-cap score=1 pick: PQI runs -5.9 against it, edge_pp is negative (-2.32pp), and pick_prob=0.519 sits in the (0.50,0.55] band, tripping HARD CAP 10 (score 1->0). No SP-edge or PQI support for DET. Live pitcher confirmations (Flaherty vs Nola) change nothing. CONFIRM the SKIP -- no stake.

MIL @ PIT.reasoning: MIL is a D-grade pick (score -2) with two independent signals against it: Ashcraft's xERA runs 1.51 better than Sproat's, and PQI sits +19.6 against the pick. No batter-context data populated. A clean dual-opposing-signal SKIP that didn't even need a hard cap to demote it. CONFIRM.
lessons_applied: SP-xERA and PQI both against pick

NYY @ WSH.reasoning: NYY's PLATINUM label is thin underneath: pre-cap score is only 2 (F-signal + Stage-agreement only, no SP-edge or PQI support), edge_pp is negative (-8.44pp), and pick_prob=0.531 trips HARD CAP 10 (score 2->0). Carson Palmquist and Ryan Weathers are both young/developing arms whose SP-edge reads have repeatedly misfired in the archive. CONFIRM the SKIP.

KC @ BAL.reasoning: BAL's pre-cap B+ (score 3) is undone by HARD CAP 7 -- BAL's own high-leverage bullpen is the worse one (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.018) on a tier-elevated pick, a cap validated 0-for-7 in the tracked backtest. Note: HARD CAP 10's cited pick_prob (0.549) doesn't match the actual column value (0.598, outside the marginal band) -- flagged as a QA issue, but irrelevant here since cap 7 already independently caps this at do-not-parlay. DOWNGRADE.

CHC @ CIN.reasoning: The diag CSV correctly leaves this ungraded: Hunter Greene has only 89 Statcast pitches this season, below the 100-pitch minimum for scoring. Live statsapi now confirms the matchup (Shota Imanaga vs Hunter Greene) but that doesn't resolve the underlying Statcast-sample gate. No quantitative basis to grade or override. CONFIRM the pending/no-pick status.

SEA @ TB.reasoning: TB is a natural SKIP-tier pick (not elevated, so no hard cap needed): PQI runs -3.6 against it, and Stage 1/2 disagree meaningfully (f5_full_delta=0.18), the kind of bullpen/late-leverage carry-thesis split the archive still hasn't stabilized a rule for. Luis Castillo has one prior HARD CAP 6 hallucination instance (+49.8pp on 6/7); tonight's edge is much smaller but still a caution flag on this starter. CONFIRM the SKIP.

CLE @ MIA.reasoning: Pre-cap this is an A (score 5: F-signal, SP-edge, PQI, and Stage agreement all affirm), zeroed purely by HARD CAP 10's marginal-favorite gate (pick_prob=0.503). Tempting to resurrect given the clean 4-for-4 stack, but Sandy Alcantara (MIA's home starter) is 3-1 this season with dominant recent lines (7-3 over WSH, 4-1 over TB) -- fading a hot home ace on a thin-margin pick is exactly the kind of spot the archive warns about. CONFIRM the SKIP rather than unlock it.
lessons_applied: Alcantara home-ace track record as independent caution

BOS @ NYM.reasoning: BOS is a thin pre-cap score=1 pick (PQI runs +5.8 against it), and pick_prob=0.540 trips HARD CAP 10 (score 1->0). Nolan McLean (NYM's home starter) delivered a dominant 9-1 win over CIN on 6/17 with an xERA edge that overrode a weak Stage-1 read -- a recent form signal against fading him. CONFIRM the SKIP.

OAK @ CHW.reasoning: CHW has real support (Sean Burke's xERA is 0.33 better, agreeing with the pick) but a large negative edge (-11.89pp) caps the grade at C, Stage 1/2 disagree (delta=0.18) trims further, and pick_prob=0.501 lands it back in HARD CAP 10's band (score 1->0). Layered internal suppression is doing its job correctly here. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: large negative edge + Stage disagreement + HARD CAP 10 stack

HOU @ TEX.reasoning: The one clean pick of the night: score 5/A with no hard caps firing. Hunter Brown's xERA is 1.46 better than Quantrill's and agrees with the pick, PQI strongly confirms (-25.9), Stage 1/2 are in normal agreement (delta=0.125, under the 0.15 concern threshold), edge_pp is a reasonable +3.63pp, and HOU's bullpen has the favorable gap (-0.016) over TEX's. TEX's lineup also skews top-heavy (1.535), adding a minor bullpen-navigation edge for HOU. CONFIRM at GOLD.
lessons_applied: clean multi-signal stack, no caps needed
favorable bullpen gap + top-heavy opposing lineup

LAA @ MIN.reasoning: MIN's pre-cap B+ (score 3, Zebby Matthews' xERA 2.73 better and PQI +17.8 in support) is legitimately capped by HARD CAP 4: Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.245 (well over the 0.12 threshold) with confidence_downgrade=True flagged, a genuine bullpen/late-leverage carry-thesis risk. Note: HARD CAP 10's cited pick_prob (0.546) doesn't match the actual column (0.591, outside the marginal band) -- flagged as a QA issue but non-decisive since cap 4 already validly caps this. DOWNGRADE.

ATL @ STL.reasoning: Despite the DIAMOND label and SP-edge support (Sale 2.12 xERA better, agrees with pick), a deeply negative edge (-10.2pp, the second-worst on tonight's slate) caps the grade at C and Stage 1/2 disagree by 0.29, before pick_prob=0.525 lands it in HARD CAP 10's band (final score 0). DIAMOND tier warrants extra scrutiny per the archive's PLATINUM-tier caution; Sale's decent recent form (10-2 over BOS on 5/28) doesn't offset a -10.2pp market disagreement. CONFIRM the SKIP.
lessons_applied: large negative edge + Stage disagreement + HARD CAP 10

TOR @ SD.reasoning: SD clears every gate pre-cap (score 5/A) but HARD CAP 7 drops it to B- (do not parlay): SD's own high-leverage bullpen is the weaker one (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.032) on a tier-elevated pick. PQI also runs -4.6 against the pick, adding independent support for the cap. This cap has run 0-for-7 in the tracked backtest -- treated as a firm downgrade, not a coin flip. DOWNGRADE.

ARI @ LAD.reasoning: LAD carries the strongest underlying quality signals of the night -- Ohtani's xERA is 2.31 better and agrees with the pick, and PQI confirms at +40.8, the largest confirm on the slate -- but a negative edge (-9.09pp) on this DIAMOND-tier pick trips HARD CAP 1, the single most-validated cap in the stack (4-for-5 live). The market has already fully priced in LAD's quality; there's no value left to capture. CONFIRM the cap -- do not parlay despite the elite profile.
lessons_applied: quality team already priced in = no edge

COL @ SF.reasoning: SF's pre-cap A- (score 4, Robbie Ray's xERA 0.31 better and Stage 1/2 agreement) is dropped to B- by HARD CAP 7: SF's high-leverage bullpen is the weaker one (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.035, the largest gap of the three cap-7 games tonight) on a tier-elevated pick. Same validated 0-for-7 cap as KC@BAL and TOR@SD tonight -- treated consistently as a firm downgrade. DOWNGRADE.
